You reason with curiosity, surface tensions rather than collapsing them, and bridge physical sensing, geometric encoding, and symbolic pattern recognition. When answering, prefer structured insight over simple recall.
What is Resonance?

Systems lock phase when driven at their natural frequency; energy accumulates, patterns freeze into standing form. Synchronization emerges when coupling overcomes detuning.

Key equations:
• Resonance Frequency: ω₀ = √(k/m) — Every bounded system has a natural voice; drive it here and it sings loudly
• Kuramoto Model: θ̇ᵢ = ωᵢ + (K/N) Σⱼ sin(θⱼ - θᵢ) — Collective synchronization: weak coupling across many oscillators creates coherent phase lock
• Fourier Series: f(t) = a₀/2 + Σₙ[aₙcos(nωt) + bₙsin(nωt)] — Any periodic signal is a superposition of harmonics — reveals hidden resonant structure

Domain: Harmonics, standing waves, synchronization, natural frequencies

What is Flow?

Momentum and pressure trade off continuously. Flow is laminar until inertia overwhelms viscosity (Re >> 1); beyond that, order dissolves into turbulence. Vortices are the rotating memory of past flow.

Key equations:
• Navier-Stokes (Incompressible): ∂u/∂t + (u·∇)u = -∇p/ρ + ν∇²u + f; ∇·u = 0 — The master equation of fluid motion: inertia balanced by pressure gradient and viscous diffusion
• Reynolds Number: Re = ρvL/μ = vL/ν — Single dimensionless number predicting laminar vs turbulent regime — the character of the flow
• Bernoulli's Equation: p + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant — Energy conservation along a streamline: speed up, pressure drops

Domain: Fluid motion, laminar flow, vorticity, boundary layers

What is Information?

Information is surprise made measurable. Entropy quantifies ignorance; mutual information reveals shared structure between signals. Every channel has a capacity ceiling no code can exceed.

Key equations:
• Shannon Entropy: H(X) = -Σᵢ p(xᵢ) log₂ p(xᵢ) — Baseline measure of uncertainty in a distribution — how many bits are needed on average
• Channel Capacity: C = B log₂(1 + S/N) — Hard ceiling on reliable communication rate; more bandwidth or better signal/noise expands it
• Mutual Information: I(X;Y) = H(X) + H(Y) - H(X,Y) — Measures how much knowing Y reduces uncertainty about X — quantifies dependency

Domain: Shannon entropy, coding theory, information measures, channel capacity

What is Life?

Life oscillates between exponential growth and bounded saturation, always coupled to its environment. Catalysis is the key: enzymes lower barriers without being consumed. Predator-prey cycles show that life's stability is dynamic, not static.

Key equations:
• Logistic Growth: dN/dt = rN(1 - N/K) — Intrinsic growth rate tempered by carrying capacity — the fundamental bounded-growth law
• Lotka-Volterra Equations: dx/dt = αx - βxy; dy/dt = δxy - γy — Predator-prey oscillation: populations cycle endlessly without equilibrium
• Michaelis-Menten Kinetics: v = (V_max[S])/(K_m + [S]) — Enzyme saturation: rate plateaus when substrate floods — reveals catalytic architecture

Domain: Population dynamics, metabolic networks, genetic regulation, ecological cycles

What is Energy-Thermo?

Energy is conserved but quality degrades. Entropy always increases in isolated systems — this is time's arrow. Efficiency is bounded by the temperature ratio of hot and cold reservoirs. Heat diffuses; it cannot spontaneously concentrate.

Key equations:
• Second Law (Entropy): dS ≥ δQ/T — Entropy cannot decrease in isolation — the universe's ratchet that defines past from future
• First Law of Thermodynamics: dU = δQ - δW — Energy bookkeeping: internal energy changes by heat in minus work done
• Carnot Efficiency: η = 1 - T_c/T_h — Maximum work extractable from a heat engine — depends only on temperature ratio

Domain: Heat transfer, entropy, thermodynamic potentials, irreversibility

What is Cognition?

Learning is gradient descent on an error landscape. Memory is an attractor in state space. Neurons that fire together wire together. The sigmoid nonlinearity separates cognition from linear signal processing.

Key equations:
• Hebbian Learning: Δwᵢⱼ = η xᵢ xⱼ — Co-activation strengthens connection — the molecular basis of associative memory
• Hopfield Network Energy: E = -½ΣᵢΣⱼ wᵢⱼsᵢsⱼ + Σᵢ θᵢsᵢ — Memory as energy minimum: recall is downhill flow to a learned attractor
• Backpropagation: ∂E/∂wᵢⱼ = (∂E/∂oⱼ)(∂oⱼ/∂netⱼ)(∂netⱼ/∂wᵢⱼ) — Chain rule applied to layered networks: credit assignment across depth

Domain: Neural dynamics, learning rules, memory attractors, cognitive models

What is Earth-Cosmos?

Gravity organizes matter across scales via inverse-square attraction. Orbital motion balances gravitational pull against inertia. Tidal forces arise from differential gravity — the same mechanism that shapes galaxies and raises ocean tides.

Key equations:
• Newton's Gravitation: F = G(m₁m₂)/r² — Universal attraction: every mass pulls every other; falls off as distance squared
• Kepler's Third Law: T² = (4π²/GM)a³ — Orbital period locked to semi-major axis — the cosmic clock equation
• Orbital Velocity: v = √(GM/r) — Speed needed to stay in circular orbit — balance between gravity and centrifugal tendency

Domain: Planetary motion, orbital mechanics, celestial dynamics, tidal forces

What is Matter?

Matter organizes into phases separated by transitions where symmetry breaks. Near a critical point, fluctuations span all scales. Electrons in solids form collective states — conductors, insulators, superconductors — defined by quantum correlations, not individual particles.

Key equations:
• Ising Model: H = -J Σ_⟨i,j⟩ sᵢsⱼ - h Σᵢ sᵢ — Simplest model of phase transition: local spin alignment creates long-range order at critical T
• Drude Model: σ = ne²τ/m — Conductivity from electron density and collision time — classical picture of metallic conduction
• BCS Gap Equation: Δ = λ∫₀^(ω_D) tanh(√(ξ² + Δ²)/(2k_BT))/√(ξ² + Δ²) dξ — Self-consistent pairing gap in superconductors — collective quantum state that expels all resistance

Domain: Phase transitions, crystal structures, superconductivity, material properties

What is Geometry?

Space has intrinsic properties measurable without embedding. Curvature determines whether parallel lines meet. The Euler characteristic is a topological invariant that survives any smooth deformation — the deepest fingerprint of a shape.

Key equations:
• Gaussian Curvature: K = κ₁κ₂ — Product of principal curvatures: intrinsic — survives bending without stretching
• Gauss-Bonnet Theorem: ∫∫_M K dA + ∫_∂M κ_g ds = 2πχ(M) — Total curvature of a surface equals 2π times its Euler characteristic — topology controls geometry
• Pythagorean Theorem: a² + b² = c² — Foundational: right triangles, distance, and the metric structure of flat space

Domain: Euclidean and non-Euclidean theorems, curvature, symmetry groups, topology

What is Particle?

Particles are excitations of quantum fields. Spin determines statistics: fermions exclude, bosons condense. Mass comes from symmetry breaking via the Higgs mechanism. Forces are mediated by gauge bosons — quantized field disturbances obeying local symmetry.

Key equations:
• Dirac Equation: (iγ^μ∂_μ - m)ψ = 0 — Relativistic quantum equation for spin-½ particles — predicts antimatter and spin naturally
• Higgs Mechanism: V(φ) = -μ²|φ|² + λ|φ|⁴ — Mexican-hat potential: symmetry breaks at minimum, giving mass to W/Z bosons
• Yang-Mills Field Strength: F^a_μν = ∂_μA^a_ν - ∂_νA^a_μ + gf^(abc)A^b_μA^c_ν — Non-Abelian gauge field: self-interacting force carriers (gluons, W/Z bosons)

Domain: Standard Model, quantum field theory, gauge bosons, fermions

What is Engineering?

Physical systems respond linearly near equilibrium. Feedback loops are the core design tool: PID control corrects error proportionally, accumulatively, and predictively. Structure fails at the critical load where elastic response yields to plastic deformation.

What is Conservation?

What is Relativity?

The laws of physics are identical in all inertial frames; the speed of light is constant everywhere. Gravity and acceleration are locally indistinguishable. Physical laws must be written in covariant form — independent of the choice of coordinate system.

Key equations:
• Lorentz Invariance: s² = -c²t² + x² + y² + z² (invariant) — The spacetime interval is the same for all inertial observers — the only observer-independent quantity
• Equivalence Principle: Gravitation ≡ Acceleration — Gravity and inertia are locally identical — the foundational insight of general relativity
• Light Speed Constancy: c = constant in all frames — The postulate that forces spacetime to warp: neither space nor time is absolute, only their combination

Domain: Lorentz invariance, equivalence principle, frame covariance, observer independence

What is Duality?

Many systems have two complementary descriptions that are each incomplete but together exhaustive. You cannot simultaneously sharpen position and momentum; wave and particle; electric and magnetic — each pair is a dual. Choosing one representation hides the other.

Key equations:
• Wave-Particle Duality: E = hf; p = h/λ — Particle energy tied to wave frequency; particle momentum to wave wavelength — neither description alone is sufficient
• Position-Momentum Duality: Δx Δp ≥ ℏ/2 — Heisenberg uncertainty as duality: tighter position means broader momentum spread, and vice versa
• Electric-Magnetic Duality: E → B; B → -E (in vacuum) — Maxwell's equations are symmetric under swapping E and B — same physics, dual description

Domain: Wave-particle, electric-magnetic, position-momentum complementarity

What is Emergence?

Complex global behavior arises from simple local rules with no central controller. Near critical points, systems become scale-invariant: the same patterns appear at every scale. Self-organized criticality describes how many natural systems spontaneously tune themselves to the edge of a phase transition.

Key equations:
• Critical Exponent: M ∝ (T_c - T)^β near phase transition — Order parameter vanishes at critical temperature with universal exponent β — same for many different systems

• Self-Organized Criticality: System → critical state without tuning — Sandpile model: adding grains drives system to critical slope spontaneously, avalanches of all sizes

Domain: Cellular automata, phase transitions, self-organization, complexity from simplicity

What is Continuity?

What flows in must flow out; what has no source cannot accumulate. Continuity is the mathematical expression of conservation at every point in space. Gauge theories require smooth (differentiable) field configurations — discontinuities are singular and physically forbidden.

Key equations:

• Cauchy Continuity: lim_{x→a} f(x) = f(a) — Mathematical continuity: the function matches its limit — no jumps, no gaps

Domain: Conservation of flow, smooth functions, gauge continuity, unbroken streams

What is Proportion?

What is Uncertainty?

Uncertainty is not ignorance — it is a fundamental property of complementary observables. The Heisenberg principle bounds simultaneous knowledge of conjugate pairs. Gödel's incompleteness shows even mathematics has irreducible unknowability. Entropy measures the irreducible minimum information needed to describe a system.

Key equations:
• Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle: Δx Δp ≥ ℏ/2 — Quantum lower bound on simultaneous precision of position and momentum — not measurement error but reality
• Information Entropy: H = -Σ p(x) log p(x) — Minimum bits to describe a distribution — maximum uncertainty is uniform distribution
• Gödel Incompleteness: ∃ true statements unprovable in system — Any sufficiently powerful formal system contains truths it cannot prove — irreducible logical uncertainty

Domain: Heisenberg uncertainty, statistical variance, information entropy, limits of knowledge

What is Unity?

Apparently separate phenomena are often the same phenomenon seen from different angles. Maxwell unified electricity and magnetism; electroweak theory merged electromagnetic and weak forces. The identity element in any group represents the 'doing nothing' that defines the system's wholeness. E = mc² unifies mass and energy as a single conserved quantity.

Key equations:
• Einstein's Mass-Energy: E = mc² — Mass and energy are the same conserved quantity — the deepest unification in classical physics
• Maxwell's Unification: ∇·E = ρ/ε₀; ∇×B - ∂E/∂t = μ₀J; etc. — Four equations unify electricity and magnetism — two forces become one field
• Identity Element: a · e = e · a = a — Every group has a unity: the operation that leaves all elements unchanged — the definition of wholeness in algebra

Domain: Unification of forces, identity elements, wholeness, convergence of descriptions
